evidence.scientific_clarity: Log-scale loss curves with CI bands for 3 methods over 50 steps; double-headed arrow highlights the Baseline–Ours gap at final step, directly matching the brief's claim.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype are never set; PDF embeds DejaVuSans as Type 3 (non-selectable paths); default DejaVu Sans used instead of a preferred journal font.
evidence.palette: Red (#D65F5F) and green (#6ACC65) are used together for two of three methods, which is not safe for deuteranopes/protanopes; no colorblind-safe palette (e.g. Okabe-Ito) applied.
evidence.layout_density: 5.5×3.8 in figure with tight_layout; log y-axis well-ranged; legend upper-right; annotation placed outside axes with clip_on=False; no wasted whitespace or label overlap.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and includes Δ=0.35 numerical claim and n=5 seeds; however red/green palette and non-editable text would likely draw Reviewer 2 comments.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is genuine vector (1-page PDF 1.4) but font is Type 3 (BMQQDV+DejaVuSans, outlined glyphs) — text is not selectable or editable; fonttype=42 was not set.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial (PDF 16 KB, PNG 76 KB, SVG 36 KB).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states the finding ('Ours converges fastest'), quantifies the gap (Δ=0.35), identifies the comparison point (final step), and reports sample size (n=5 seeds).

overall_strengths: Well-structured single-panel layout with log scale, CI shading, and annotated gap arrow that directly addresses the brief's scientific claim.
Caption is publication-ready: specific numerical finding, sample size, and clear description of visual elements.
overall_weaknesses: Red+green color pair is not colorblind-safe and carries implicit directional connotation; should be replaced with a perceptually uniform, colorblind-safe palette (e.g. Okabe-Ito or viridis-derived).
pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype='none') are not set, so all text in the PDF is rendered as Type 3 outlined paths — non-selectable, non-editable, and will fail many journal submission checks.
